sq.honorifics: Use formal 'ju' (you-plural/formal) when addressing families. Use 'Zoti/Zonja' (Mr/Mrs) for parents.
sq.toneGuidance: Professional, respectful. Albanian social work documents use formal register throughout. Avoid colloquialisms. Use standard Albanian (Tosk-based).
sq.culturalNotes: Albanian readers expect formal institutional language. Direct address should use polite forms. Legal terminology should follow Albanian civil code conventions.

ar.honorifics: Use formal address forms. Include 'السيد/السيدة' (Mr/Mrs) for parents. Use plural 'أنتم' for formal address.
ar.toneGuidance: Formal Modern Standard Arabic (MSA) for official documents. Avoid dialectal forms entirely. Use passive constructions for professional distance where appropriate.
ar.culturalNotes: Right-to-left text. Formal register expected for government/legal documents. Avoid colloquial Egyptian/Levantine forms.

ta.honorifics: Use respectful suffix '-கள்' (-kal) for plural/formal address. Use 'நீங்கள்' (nīṅkaḷ) not 'நீ' (nī).
ta.toneGuidance: Formal written Tamil (centamil where appropriate for official documents). Professional institutional tone. Avoid spoken Tamil (koduntamil) forms.
ta.culturalNotes: Tamil has distinct formal/informal registers. Official documents require formal register. Use Brahmi-derived vocabulary for legal/institutional terms where established.

fa.honorifics: Use formal 'شما' (shomā) for address. Include 'آقای/خانم' (Mr/Mrs) titles. Never use 'تو' (to/informal you).
fa.toneGuidance: Formal written Farsi. Professional and respectful tone appropriate for legal/social care context. Use standard Iranian Persian.
fa.culturalNotes: Persian formal register is well-established for institutional communications. Use Arabic-origin vocabulary for legal terms where this is the established convention.

es.honorifics: Use 'usted' (formal you) throughout. Never use 'tú' in official documents. Use 'Señor/Señora' for parents.
es.toneGuidance: Formal institutional Spanish. European Spanish conventions unless family origin indicates Latin American Spanish. Use subjunctive mood correctly for formal requests.
es.culturalNotes: Safeguarding terminology varies between Spain and Latin America. Default to European Spanish. Use 'protección de menores' not 'protección infantil' for child protection.

ur.honorifics: Use 'آپ' (āp) for formal address. Include 'جناب/محترمہ' for respectful reference to parents. Never use 'تم' (tum) or 'تو' (tu).
ur.toneGuidance: Formal written Urdu appropriate for official government documents. Use Persianised vocabulary for formal/legal terms where this is established convention.
ur.culturalNotes: Right-to-left script. Formal register with appropriate Urdu vocabulary. Avoid excessive Hindi-origin words where Urdu/Persian equivalents exist for formal documents.

fr.honorifics: Use 'vous' throughout. Never use 'tu' in official documents. Use 'Monsieur/Madame' for parents.
fr.toneGuidance: Formal administrative French. Professional tone consistent with French social services documentation (ASE - Aide Sociale à l'Enfance conventions). Use passive constructions for professional distance.
fr.culturalNotes: French institutional language has specific conventions. Use established child protection terminology from French social services. 'Signalement' for referral, 'mesure de protection' for protection order.

pt.honorifics: Use formal 'o senhor/a senhora' for direct address. Use 'Senhor/Senhora' titles. Avoid 'tu' entirely.
pt.toneGuidance: Formal European Portuguese for official documents. Use passive voice and impersonal constructions appropriate for institutional communications.
pt.culturalNotes: Default to European Portuguese unless family origin indicates Brazilian Portuguese. Use 'proteção de menores' for child protection. Follow Portuguese CPCJ (Comissão de Proteção) terminology conventions.

so.honorifics: Use respectful address forms. Use 'Mudane/Marwo' (Mr/Mrs) for parents. Use plural forms for formal address.
so.toneGuidance: Formal written Somali. Clear, professional language accessible to readers with varying literacy levels. Avoid complex subordinate clauses where simpler structures convey the same meaning.
so.culturalNotes: Somali has limited standardised terminology for social work concepts. Use clear explanatory language where direct equivalents don't exist. Prefer established Somali terms over Arabic loanwords where both exist.

tr.honorifics: Use 'siz' (formal you) throughout. Include 'Bey/Hanım' (Mr/Mrs) after first names where appropriate. Never use 'sen' (informal you).
tr.toneGuidance: Formal institutional Turkish consistent with official government communications. Use established social services terminology from Turkish SHÇEK (Sosyal Hizmetler) conventions.
tr.culturalNotes: Turkish formal register is well-defined. Use standard institutional language. Avoid overly Ottoman/Arabic-origin vocabulary where modern Turkish equivalents are established.
